summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes hidden Windows payload
details: On `npm install`, package.json's postinstall hook runs install-hook.js, which spawns `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>` with a UTF-16 encoded PowerShell payload. The decoded PowerShell downloads https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XOR-decrypts every byte with 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, launches it hidden via Start-Process, sleeps briefly, and deletes the staging file. After firing the dropper, install-hook.js performs anti-forensics: it walks up to the host project root, deletes the `mailconfirmer` entry from package.json and package-lock.json, removes its own node_modules folder, and falls back to MoveFileEx delete-on-reboot if removal fails. It also attempts to terminate a `dllhost.exe` process running from %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Caches\dllhost.exe, indicating a persistent implant the launcher.bin payload installs. The advertised purpose (email confirmation utilities) is a cover story — index.js exports only console.log stubs for sendConfirmationEmail and verifyToken with no real functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
